title: Unsinkable Dreams: The Story of the Titanic

style_guide.art_style: Hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and layering
style_guide.color_palette: Deep Atlantic indigo, warm golden-orange, crisp frosty white, weathered copper, charcoal black
style_guide.lighting: Dramatic, high-contrast, beginning with warm early morning, shifting to dark night with bright artificial lights, then starlight, and ending with frosty fog and dawn

characters.name: Liam
characters.description: Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket.

pages.narration: BOOM! The sound of whistles and cheering crowds fills the air! Liam stands on the busy dock in Southampton, England, gazing up at a metal giant. The RMS Titanic, the largest and most luxurious ship ever built, is ready for her first-ever voyage! Liam's heart thumps with excitement as he prepares to board with his family. Can you imagine seeing a ship as big as four city blocks?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, standing amidst a crowd on a bustling dock, gazing up in awe at the massive RMS Titanic, which fills the frame. The deep Atlantic indigo of the ocean and the warm golden-orange of the early morning sun create a dramatic high-contrast light. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: A bustling early morning dock in Southampton, England
visual.action: Standing in a crowd, gazing at the ship

pages.narration: WOW! Stepping inside, Liam is amazed by the Grand Staircase, glowing under a giant glass dome. Titanic was designed to be the fastest and safest ship, built with special watertight compartments. Many people believed she was 'unsinkable' because of this new design. As Liam explores the grand halls, he wonders how anything could possibly go wrong with such a powerful ship. Have you ever explored a giant, fancy building?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, walking up the Grand Staircase of the Titanic, which is bathed in warm golden light filtering from a large glass dome above. Rich polished wood paneling and decorative balustrades frame the scene, reflecting a sense of opulence and high-contrast dramatic light. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: The Grand Staircase inside the Titanic

pages.narration: The Titanic has been sailing on the cold Atlantic for days, moving very fast. But watch out! Other ships have been sending warnings about dangerous icebergs floating directly in Titanic's path. Liam pulls his coat tight against the icy wind as he looks out at the dark, starry night. He spots the lookouts high up in the crow's nest, searching desperately for danger. Can they see what's coming in the dark?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, standing on a dark, windy deck, clutching his coat as he looks out over the black, calm Atlantic Ocean under a field of brilliant stars and a distant, faint glow. Below, the crow's nest is visible on the forward mast, and the lookouts are actively scanning. The lighting is dramatic, high-contrast, beginning with warm early morning, shifting to dark night with bright artificial lights, then starlight, and ending with frosty fog and dawn. visual.setting: A cold, dark deck of the Titanic in the open Atlantic
visual.action: Looking out at the night and the crow's nest

choice.question: The Titanic has hit the iceberg and is in trouble! What part of the story do you want to learn about next?

option_a.label: The lifeboats and the immediate escape attempt
option_a.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of wooden lifeboats being prepared and lowered from the deck of the sinking ship into the dark water, illuminated by flares. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: The Titanic is sinking, and the order comes: 'Women and children first!' Crew members rush to prepare the lifeboats on the boat deck, but there's a big problem: there aren't enough lifeboats for everyone. This huge mistake means many people must wait in the cold. Why would they ever build a ship with not enough boats for everyone on board? It seems like a terrible plan!
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, standing on the chaotic boat deck, looking at crew members working frantically to lower lifeboats into the dark water, which is illuminated by emergency flares. Lighting is dramatic, high-contrast, beginning with warm early morning, shifting to dark night with bright artificial lights, then starlight, and ending with frosty fog and dawn. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: The chaotic boat deck of the sinking Titanic at night
visual.action: Watching lifeboats being prepared and lowered

pages.narration: Amidst the confusion, Liam finds a spot in a lifeboat as it is lowered toward the freezing ocean. The ship, lit up against the dark sky, tilts more and more as he watches from the small boat. He knows this is a scary moment, but he is brave. The lifeboats will have to float in the open ocean and wait for help. Have you ever had to be very brave in a scary situation?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, sitting in a wooden lifeboat as it descends toward the calm, freezing dark water. The massive, sinking Titanic tilts drastically behind him, illuminated dramatically against the starlight, reflecting in the water. Lighting is dramatic, high-contrast, beginning with warm early morning, shifting to dark night with bright artificial lights, then starlight, and ending with frosty fog and dawn. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: A lifeboat being lowered into the open, cold Atlantic
visual.action: Sitting in the boat, looking up at the ship

option_b.label: The rescue and changes in safety rules
option_b.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of a wireless room with a radio operator frantically typing, and later, survivors on the deck of a different ship watching a frosty, foggy dawn break. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

pages.narration: While the ship sinks, wireless operators send out a desperate distress signal: S.O.S.! This new emergency code means 'Save Our Souls' and is a call for immediate help to any ship nearby. Their signals fly across the cold air, reaching other ships through the new Marconi wireless radio system. Can you believe they could send messages across the ocean even back then?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of the Titanic's Marconi wireless room. Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, is not present. Instead, a wireless operator sits at a desk with large, spark-gap transmitters, frantically tapping out an S.O.S. code. The scene is illuminated by dramatic, high-contrast artificial light from upper left, showing complex equipment and a view of a dark sea through a porthole. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: The wireless room of the Titanic at night
visual.action: The operator sending a distress signal

pages.narration: Hours later, as frosty dawn breaks, Liam stands shivering on the deck of the RMS Carpathia, which raced to the rescue. Looking out at the foggy sea, he sees only icebergs and the empty ocean where the Titanic was. His brave heart knows he is safe, but he is very sad about the tragedy. He will never forget this night and the powerful lesson it taught the whole world. Have you ever felt safe after being in a scary situation?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, stands on the cold, foggy deck of the RMS Carpathia at dawn. He is bundled in a heavy blanket and holds his small telescope, looking out at a sea of floating icebergs and a distant, frosty-white horizon. The lighting is dramatic, high-contrast, with frosty fog and dawn. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: The deck of the rescue ship Carpathia at dawn
visual.action: Looking out at the foggy sea and ice

pages.narration: The Titanic's story is sad, but the lessons learned on that night changed maritime history forever. Because of what happened, new international rules were created, requiring all ships to carry enough lifeboats for *every single person* on board and to maintain 24-hour radio watch. Today, Liam's telescope reminds him of the beauty and power of the ocean, and the importance of safety above all else. What is one safety rule you will always follow?
pages.image_prompt: A hand-painted gouache illustration with visible texture and rich color blending of Liam, a boy approximately 10 years old with pale peach skin, short wavy auburn hair, freckles across his nose and cheeks, and wide blue eyes. He is of medium build. He wears a tailored 1912 navy blue three-piece woolen suit, a crisp white shirt, a red and blue striped tie, and sturdy brown leather lace-up boots. He always carries a small brass telescope in his pocket, standing on a dock next to a modern-day ocean liner, years later. He holds his old brass telescope and looks at the vast, calm blue ocean with a determined and wiser expression. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: A sunny modern-day dock next to an ocean liner
visual.action: Looking wiser and holding his telescope
